texts.label: text
texts.orig: Newly generated sequences are indicated in bold. *N/A, data not available in GenBank.
texts.text: Newly generated sequences are indicated in bold. *N/A, data not available in GenBank.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Maximum likelihood (ML) analyses were performed using IQ-TREE web server (Trifinopoulos et al., 2016). Substitution model was automatically tested. Ultrafast bootstrap (BS) analysis was implemented with 1,000 replicates. Maximum likelihood bootstrap values (ML-BS) equal or greater than 75% are marked near each node.
texts.text: Maximum likelihood (ML) analyses were performed using IQ-TREE web server (Trifinopoulos et al., 2016). Substitution model was automatically tested. Ultrafast bootstrap (BS) analysis was implemented with 1,000 replicates. Maximum likelihood bootstrap values (ML-BS) equal or greater than 75% are marked near each node.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Bayesian inference (BI) analyses were carried out in MrBayes 3.2.6 (Ronquist et al., 2012) using a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm. The best-fit substitution model GRT +I+ G was decided for all four gene regions by MrModeltest 2.3 (Nylander, 2008) under the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC). Two parallel runs of four simultaneous Markov chains were performed for 1,000,000 generations. Trees were sampled every 1,000th generations. Burn-in phase was set at 25% and the remaining trees were used for calculating posterior probabilities (PP). PP values equal or greater than 0.95 are marked near each node.
texts.text: Bayesian inference (BI) analyses were carried out in MrBayes 3.2.6 (Ronquist et al., 2012) using a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm. The best-fit substitution model GRT +I+ G was decided for all four gene regions by MrModeltest 2.3 (Nylander, 2008) under the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC). Two parallel runs of four simultaneous Markov chains were performed for 1,000,000 generations. Trees were sampled every 1,000th generations. Burn-in phase was set at 25% and the remaining trees were used for calculating posterior probabilities (PP). PP values equal or greater than 0.95 are marked near each node.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Trees were visualized with FigTree v1.4.4, and the layouts were edited using Adobe Illustrator CS6 software (Adobe Systems, USA).
texts.text: Trees were visualized with FigTree v1.4.4, and the layouts were edited using Adobe Illustrator CS6 software (Adobe Systems, USA).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hermatomyces hainanensis J. Ma, Y.Z. Zhang & Y.Z. Lu, sp. nov., Figure 1.
texts.text: Hermatomyces hainanensis J. Ma, Y.Z. Zhang & Y.Z. Lu, sp. nov., Figure 1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Etymology: Referring to the location where the species was collected.
texts.text: Etymology: Referring to the location where the species was collected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Saprobic on decaying wood in freshwater habitat. Sexual morph: Undetermined. Asexual morph: hyphomycetous. Colonies on natural substrate sporodochial, effuse, scattered, circular or subcircular, consisting of a brown sterile mycelial outer zone and an abundantly sporulating, dark brown to blackish brown center. Mycelium partly immersed, partly superficial, composed of pale to brown, branched, septate hyphae. Conidiophores 15-19x um (r= 48 × 32 pm, n= n=15), micronematous to semi-macronematous, unevenly cylindrical, geniculate, subhyaline to brown, septate, thick-walled. Conidiogenous cells 4.5-8×2.5-3um (x=6.5×2.5um, n= 15), monoblastic, integrated, terminal, subcylindrical, subhyaline to pale brown. Conidia dimorphic, (1) cylindrical conidia 51-67 × 16-24 um (x = 58.5 × 21 um, n=30), hyaline to subhyaline, often with a distinct dark brown pigmentation from the top downwards or at rim of the conidia, straight or broadly curved, phragmoseptate or muriform, sometimes with oblique septa, constricted at the septa, consisting of two columns from one or two basal cells, rounded at the apex; (2) lenticular conidia 44-52 × 29-39 um (x=48 × 32 um, n= 30), ellipsoidal in front view, central cells dark brown to blackish brown, peripheral cells subhyaline to pale olivaceous brown, forming a distinct ring, muriform, constricted at the septa, smooth-walled, side views not observed.
texts.text: Saprobic on decaying wood in freshwater habitat. Sexual morph: Undetermined. Asexual morph: hyphomycetous. Mycelium partly immersed, partly superficial, composed of pale to brown, branched, septate hyphae. Conidiophores 15-19x um (r= 48 × 32 pm, n= n=15), micronematous to semi-macronematous, unevenly cylindrical, geniculate, subhyaline to brown, septate, thick-walled. Conidiogenous cells 4.5-8×2.5-3um (x=6.5×2.5um, n= 15), monoblastic, integrated, terminal, subcylindrical, subhyaline to pale brown. Conidia dimorphic, (1) cylindrical conidia 51-67 × 16-24 um (x = 58.5 × 21 um, n=30), hyaline to subhyaline, often with a distinct dark brown pigmentation from the top downwards or at rim of the conidia, straight or broadly curved, phragmoseptate or muriform, sometimes with oblique septa, constricted at the septa, consisting of two columns from one or two basal cells, rounded at the apex; (2) lenticular conidia 44-52 × 29-39 um (x=48 × 32 um, n= 30), ellipsoidal in front view, central cells dark brown to blackish brown, peripheral cells subhyaline to pale olivaceous brown, forming a distinct ring, muriform, constricted at the septa, smooth-walled, side views not observed.
